title: CS2 Rank Distribution & Ratings Guide
description: Explore CS2 Premier Mode rating distribution and Competitive rank percentiles. Find your rank, understand skill levels, and learn ranking strategies.
heading: CS2 Rank Distribution & Ratings
intro: Understand where you stand in the competitive landscape

faqs.question: What is Premier Mode rating in CS2?
faqs.answer: Premier Mode uses a numeric rating system (0-35,000+) that reflects your skill level and rank. Unlike the old 18-rank system, Premier Mode provides more granular ranking with continuous progression. Your rating is based on performance, win/loss ratio, and how you perform against players of different skill levels.

faqs.question: How does the rating distribution affect matchmaking?
faqs.answer: CS2 matchmaking uses rating to pair players of similar skill levels. The distribution shows most players cluster between 5,000-15,000 rating. This creates better balanced matches where both teams have comparable skill levels, leading to more competitive and enjoyable games.

faqs.question: What\'s a good rating for my rank in CS2?
faqs.answer: A "good" rating depends on your goals. 10,000+ is above average, 15,000+ is distinguished, and 20,000+ puts you in the top 15%. For competitive players aiming to improve, focus on steady progression rather than absolute numbers. Consistent play and practice matter more than rating.

faqs.question: How can I improve from my current rating?
faqs.answer: Improvement comes from: 1) Mastering spray control and crosshair placement, 2) Learning map positions and callouts, 3) Playing with a consistent team when possible, 4) Watching pro replays to improve game sense, 5) Taking breaks to avoid tilt, 6) Focusing on utility usage and positioning over kills.

faqs.question: Are these distribution percentages accurate?
faqs.answer: These are estimates based on community data and may not reflect exact current numbers. Valve does not publish official distribution data. These percentages should be used as general guidelines for understanding relative skill levels. The actual distribution may vary as the player base evolves.
